prerequisites: A Wufoo account. The account name forms the subdomain used in every API request, for example {subdomain}.wufoo.com.
Your Wufoo API key (a 16-digit code), found in the Form Manager under API Information in the More dropdown for any form.
The subdomain config field, which must be set to your Wufoo account name, since all API endpoints are served from https://{subdomain}.wufoo.com/api/v3.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Wufoo uses HTTP Basic authentication with an API key. The api_key secret field is sent as the Basic Auth username, and any value may be used as the password. Provide the 16-digit key from your account's API Information page.

setupSteps.title: Find your subdomain
setupSteps.body: Your subdomain is your Wufoo account name. It appears in your account URL ({subdomain}.wufoo.com) and is required because every API request is made to https://{subdomain}.wufoo.com/api/v3. Set the subdomain config field to this account name.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to Wufoo. From the Form Manager, open the More dropdown on any form and select API Information. Copy the 16-digit code shown there. This unique key grants access to your data, so keep it protected like a password. You can reset it from the same page if needed, but resetting it will break any existing integrations until the new key is supplied.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the 16-digit code into the api_key field and your account name into the subdomain field. The connector authenticates over HTTP Basic auth, sending the api_key as the username and any placeholder value as the password.

sources.title: Wufoo REST API v3 documentation (Authentication and API URL structure)
Using API Information and Webhooks - SurveyMonkey Help (finding and resetting the Wufoo API key)
